blocks.headline: Protect residents. Protect occupancy. Protect NOI.
blocks.subtitle: Objective falls screening for CCRCs, rental retirement, independent and assisted living. Reduce resident turnover from falls by up to 25%.

tiles.label: Clinicians
tiles.benefit: Objective metrics in the flow of care.

blocks.heading: Technology built for the frontline
blocks.body: Able Assess fits into the resident's daily routine. Five minutes in the wellness center, the dining room, or the resident's apartment. Non-clinical staff can run it. Results are available immediately.
blocks.imageAlt: Wellness director running an Able Assess screening with a resident in a senior living community

blocks.heading: How it works in senior living

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: A wellness director or care aide runs a five-minute assessment on any tablet during a routine wellness check.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go are scored against normative data instantly.

steps.title: Act
steps.description: Risk banding triggers care plan updates, level-of-care transition conversations, and family communication.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Longitudinal trends give residents objective milestones and give operators population-level insight into fall risk.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.description: Upper-body strength proxy. Predicts frailty, mortality and functional decline.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.description: Lower-limb power and balance. Key to independent mobility in IL and AL.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.description: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to fall risk and level-of-care transitions.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.description: Falls risk in under a minute. Validated across community and clinical settings.

blocks.heading: How much could you save?
blocks.promoBody: Model the financial impact of reducing falls-related turnover and liability across your communities.

blocks.heading: Common questions from senior living operators

items.question: What hardware do we need?
items.answer: A GripAble sensor and any tablet. The sensor connects via Bluetooth. No additional infrastructure required.

items.question: Can non-clinical staff run the assessment?
items.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built for non-clinical staff. Wellness directors, care aides and activities coordinators can all run the workflow with no training course.

items.question: How does it help with occupancy?
items.answer: Falls are a leading cause of resident turnover. Objective screening has been shown to reduce falls by 25%, which protects length of stay and occupancy rates.

items.question: Can residents see their own data?
items.answer: Yes. Residents get objective milestones they can track over time, which motivates participation in wellness programs and builds trust in the care plan.

items.question: How does it reduce liability?
items.answer: A standardized, documented screening protocol creates a defensible record of proactive falls risk management for every resident.

items.question: What does it cost?
items.answer: Pricing is based on community size and assessment volume. The ROI calculator models payback against reduced falls-related turnover and liability. Book a demo to discuss your specific scenario.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your community.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has run it in senior living.
